hypothesis: If silicon-based Li–Si–X compounds are selected or generated by an AI pipeline conditioned on >1500 mAh/g capacity and a largest Li+ migration channel radius that remains nearly constant between charged and discharged states, then the resulting materials will relax to thermodynamically stable structures that preserve large Li+ channels across (de)lithiation because channel-radius invariance is a reliable descriptor of structural robustness in high-capacity anodes.
